description: SVACS Vessel Knowledge Registry — deterministic acoustic classification metadata

cargo.expected_acoustic_behavior: Steady low-frequency propeller rotation. Single dominant FFT peak in 50-200Hz band. Low noise floor. Amplitude modulation minimal.
cargo.classification_note: Clean single FFT peak. Easiest vessel to classify correctly.

speedboat.expected_acoustic_behavior: High-frequency engine signature with second harmonic. Irregular amplitude from fast RPM. High noise floor from cavitation.
speedboat.classification_note: Second harmonic at 2x dominant frequency is the key identifier.

submarine.expected_acoustic_behavior: Near-infrasonic low-energy signature. Stealth masking reduces amplitude to 25-60% of unmasked value. Near ocean noise floor. Energy distinctly below cargo range.
submarine.classification_note: Overlaps with cargo at 50-100Hz. Energy threshold < 1,200,000 is the primary discriminator. Under noisy conditions may classify as unknown — this is expected and honest behavior.

low_confidence.expected_acoustic_behavior: Signal buried under heavy ocean noise. Amplitude 0.1-0.2, noise 0.5-0.8. No reliable dominant frequency. Could be distant vessel, degraded sensor, or heavy sea state.
low_confidence.classification_note: Signal-to-noise ratio too low for reliable classification. System correctly outputs unknown with low confidence — this is honest behavior, not a failure.

anomaly.expected_acoustic_behavior: Multi-frequency spike pattern with 3-7 components across non-standard bands. Random amplitude bursts at irregular positions. Does not match any known vessel propulsion profile.
anomaly.classification_note: May represent biologic event (whale), equipment malfunction, unknown vessel class, or jamming signal. Always flagged as anomaly regardless of which frequency band the dominant peak falls in.

unknown.expected_acoustic_behavior: Dominant frequency does not fall within any known vessel classification band. Could be submarine under noisy conditions, unknown vessel type, or signal artifact.
unknown.classification_note: This is the honest output when the system cannot classify. It means the signal exists but does not match known profiles — operator should investigate.
